narrative.summary: OG natural wine bar since 2012, minimalist design, 400+ bottles, 30+ by the glass - the anchor of Toronto's natty wine scene

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We had such a fun time at Archive! The we ordered 4 different wines by the glass and they were all delicious. We orecchiette dish was large and really good, and the squash cappelletti DIVINE. I recommend the kitchen to add a version with meat filling and tomato sauce. The service was super friendly and we felt welcomed and cozy."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
An unforgettable wine experience in the heart of Dundas West!

From the moment you step into Archive Wine Bar at 909 Dundas Street West — just east of Trinity Bellwoods — you’re welcomed by a warm, intimate ambiance that feels both chic and comfortably lived‑in. The interior is a sensory delight: exposed brick walls and shelves lined with bottles create a cozy, inviting glow, while mellow music and soft chatter make it perfect for everything from a romantic date night to a relaxed evening with friends.  ￼

Archive’s wine program is nothing short of phenomenal. With over 30 wines by the glass and hundreds by the bottle, this place is a dream for both curious novices and seasoned wine lovers alike. Their list leans toward carefully selected local Ontario and natural wines alongside intriguing European options, and many rare bottles are offered in generous pours. Bartenders are knowledgeable and passionate, guiding you through flavour profiles and helping tailor your picks to your palate — a true highlight.  ￼

The food menu perfectly complements the drink list. Expect thoughtfully curated small plates and tapas‑style bites — from freshly sliced charcuterie and artisanal cheeses to creative Western‑European snacks — ideal for pairing with each glass. The charcuterie board in particular is a sensory experience: fragrant, textured, and beautifully plated, with flavours that enhance every sip.  ￼

Service here strikes a lovely balance — friendly, unpretentious, and genuinely enthusiastic about wine culture. Whether you’re lingering over a long conversation or popping in for a quick tasting session, the staff make you feel at ease.  ￼

Practical details:
•   📞 Reservations recommended for weekend evenings — especially if you want a prime table. You can call ahead the day-of or fill out the online form via their website.  ￼
•   🪑 Accessibility: Street‑level entrance with no major stairs. Street parking (paid) is typical on Dundas West; early arrival helps.  ￼
•   🕒 Hours: Typically evenings and late nights — great for both early drinks and post‑dinner wind‑downs.  ￼
•   🚗 Parking: Street parking only, often metered; TTC access and nearby rideshares are convenient alternatives.  ￼

Overall vibe: Cozy yet vibrant, elegant but never stuffy. Whether you’re discovering new varietals on a date, sharing a bottle with friends, or simply tipping back a glass after work, Archive feels like a hidden nook of sophistication and warmth in Toronto’s wine scene. Highly recommended — truly five stars!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I can’t speak give a high enough review for this wine bar. First off they held a couple of spots for my wife and I on a Saturday night. The wine list is massive, and features bottles at many different prices. The wines by the glass are a great way to explore different wines. The food was amazing and the charcuterie is sliced right in front of you at the bar.  The atmosphere is exactly what you would want in a wine bar, the service is the best in the city and they have incredible knowledge. The biggest problem is that we just discovered it and it’s only a short walk from our house so we will be coming back in a regular basis. Thanks for an amazing night !"
